artifact_or_test_id: live responses: identical written list on consecutive runs; render_agent_surfaces sites ~1193-1230 (unconditional write_text + append)
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Every sync in a fresh phase credits four unchanged files; the manifest overstates what the render changed.
expected: A byte-identical wf_sync_surfaces re-run returns written: [].
known_bad_detection_method: live double-run probe; the strengthened Guru-enabled fixture fails against the unconditional writers
limitations: Credit impact bounded by replay dedup within a phase (identical request+response share an event id); the manifest truthfulness defect is the substance.
observed: Two consecutive live mode='run' calls on the reloaded server both returned the same four paths. The hermetic fixture missed it: the temp repo had no Guru surface, so render_agent_surfaces early-returned and the test exercised only the platform-module chokepoint — an incomplete-fixture blind spot.
proposition: render_agent_surfaces writes and reports its four generated tier-3 surfaces (auto-guru.mdc, guru.md, SKILL.md, config.toml) unconditionally, so 1t729's manifest lists them as written on every byte-identical re-render, violating the changed-only manifest contract (AC-3's no-op case).

disposition_rationale: Bounded repair: the module's own write_text gains change detection and the four sites append only on change, matching the module's other writers; the hermetic fixture gains a Guru-enabled repo.

command_or_fixture: python3 -B -m unittest tests.test_render_platform_surfaces.ManifestChannelTests (2 OK); scratch double-render byte-hash probe showing stable on-disk state
counterexample_or_failure_condition: The Guru-enabled fixture's empty-second-manifest assertion fails.
expected: A byte-identical re-render's manifest is empty, including the four tier-3 surfaces; a genuinely changed surface still reports.
known_bad_detection_method: the strengthened fixture fails against the pre-repair reporting
limitations: Full-suite and live double-probe confirmation attach at reverification.
observed: Investigation surfaced the real mechanism: templates are intentionally rewritten then reconciled every render (write-time comparison oscillates), and the closing reconcile's honest 'updated' return leaked excluded candidates back into the manifest. Net-change restructure applied; the strengthened Guru-enabled fixture (which reproduces the live find hermetically) passes both assertions.
proposition: Tier-3 agent surfaces report NET change across the whole render: pre-render bytes are snapshotted before the template write, the verdict is taken after the closing reconcile pass, and the reconcile pass's own return no longer re-adds candidate paths whose net verdict was unchanged.
safety_and_authorization: Repo-contained renders and hermetic tests only.

disposition_rationale: Bounded to render_agent_surfaces' manifest reporting; the render's on-disk behavior (template write + reconcile) is intentionally unchanged and verified byte-stable across renders.

command_or_fixture: operator independent static review; confirmed by code read of both sequences
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Simultaneous suite start and build start slip through and race.
expected: No interleaving of the two start sequences permits concurrent execution.
known_bad_detection_method: operator static review; new interleaving regression tests fail against the check-then-act ordering
limitations: The probe primitives are unchanged; only the ordering and tie-break discipline change.
observed: Confirmed at run_tests.py main (probe at ~line 389, acquire after) and indexer.py _index_build_lock (deferral probe at ~3060 before acquire). Existing tests cover only already-held locks, not the interleaving.
proposition: The 1t727 exclusion is check-then-act on BOTH sides: run_tests probes index-build.lock before acquiring test-run.lock, and the indexer probes test-run.lock before acquiring index-build.lock, so two simultaneous starts can each see the other's lock free, then acquire their own locks and run concurrently — the exact contention the change exists to prevent.

disposition_rationale: Bounded asymmetric repair: the build defers IN PLACE while holding its lock (post-acquire, never releases, bounded); the suite re-probes after acquiring its lock and YIELDS (release + re-wait) when a build holds — one side yields, the other holds, so the tie converges without deadlock or livelock.

command_or_fixture: python3 -B -m unittest tests.test_run_tests_lock tests.test_server_context_efficiency tests.test_indexer (344 OK)
counterexample_or_failure_condition: A source-ordering assertion fails, or a yield cycle deadlocks in the behavior tests.
expected: No interleaving of the two start sequences permits concurrent execution; ties resolve build-wins.
known_bad_detection_method: the source-ordering tests fail against the pre-repair check-then-act sequences
limitations: Full-suite confirmation attaches at reverification; the convergence argument is analytic (one yielding side) rather than a stress reproduction.
observed: Ordering pinned by source-position tests (run_tests: wait < acquire < re-probe < release; indexer: deferral strictly post-acquire with no pre-acquire call remaining) plus the existing behavior tests; focused suites 344/344 OK. Intra-process lock probing was discovered invalid during test authoring (fcntl record locks never conflict within one process), so the ordering assertions are source-level by recorded necessity.
proposition: The exclusion is now asymmetric and atomic-with-ownership: the indexer defers WHILE HOLDING the build lock (deferral moved post-acquire inside _index_build_lock), and the suite re-probes the build lock AFTER acquiring the run lock, yielding (release + re-wait, three bounded cycles) when a build holds — exactly one yielding side, so every interleaving converges without deadlock or livelock.
safety_and_authorization: Repo-contained edits and hermetic tests only.

disposition_rationale: Bounded to the two start sequences; the probe primitives are unchanged.

artifact_or_test_id: test_build_defers_unlocked_until_test_lock_releases (build lock FREE during deferral); test_final_cycle_proceeds_never_cancelled; test_deferral_ordering_is_post_acquire; test_suite_yield_source_discipline; hermetic cache-test patches
command_or_fixture: python3 run_tests.py --no-cache (6,041 OK); the failed 600s-hang suite run as the refuting reproduction; focused suites 381/381
counterexample_or_failure_condition: Any ordering assertion fails, a deferring build holds its lock during a wait, or a bounded cycle fails to terminate.
expected: acquire < atomic check < release < unlocked wait on the build side; wait < acquire < re-probe < yield on the suite side; a deferring build's lock is FREE during its wait; bounded cycles with safe endgames.
known_bad_detection_method: the refuting full-suite hang reproduced the phantom-build hazard; the revised tests fail against both the original race and the defer-while-holding draft
limitations: Convergence under adversarial perfectly-synchronized restarts is argued analytically (differing poll periods + bounded cycles with safe endgames), not stress-reproduced.
observed: The first repair draft (defer-while-holding) was itself live-refuted within the hour: a hook-spawned build deferring to the running suite presented as a phantom running build and test_run_tests_cache waited out its whole 600s budget (0 tests). Revised to hold-nothing-while-waiting; the behavior test asserts the build lock is FREE during deferral, source-ordering tests pin both sequences, cache tests are hermetically isolated from the real repository lock, and the canonical suite is 6,041/6,041 OK.
proposition: The final exclusion design closes the TOCTOU race with atomic post-acquire rechecks on both sides while NOBODY ever waits holding a lock, and the full suite is clean on the final tree.
safety_and_authorization: Repo-contained edits, hermetic tests, and one refuting suite run.

disposition_rationale: The repair was refuted once by live evidence and revised; the revised design is confirmed by behavior, ordering, and full-suite evidence.
